name: Dark 72% Nicaragua
tastingNotes: Dark cherry, toasted almond, lingering cocoa
description: A bold single‑origin bar from the volcanic soils of Nicaragua. Intense and complex, with a long, satisfying finish.
process: Slow-fermented for 6 days, stone-ground for 72 hours, hand-tempered in small batches.
pairing: Pairs perfectly with a bold, aged Cabernet Sauvignon or a medium-roast single-origin espresso.
testimonial: The depth of the volcanic soil is unmistakable; a truly profound experience for the dark chocolate purist.

name: Milk 45% Madagascar
tastingNotes: Caramel, vanilla, creamy milk chocolate with red fruit undertones
description: Smooth and fruity with a hint of red fruit. Made with heirloom cacao from the Sambirano Valley.
process: Slow-fermented for 5 days, stone-ground for 48 hours, delicately tempered.
pairing: Pairs wonderfully with a fruity Pinot Noir or a creamy cappuccino.
testimonial: The subtle fruit notes dance on the palate – a masterpiece of milk chocolate.

name: White 33% Ecuador
tastingNotes: Honey, milk, white flower, silky vanilla
description: Sweet and delicate with a silky texture. Made with single‑origin cocoa butter from the Amazon basin.
process: Gently conched for 36 hours, hand-poured into small moulds.
pairing: Pairs beautifully with a glass of Moscato d'Asti or fresh raspberries.
testimonial: A delicate floral sweetness that lingers – unlike any white chocolate I've ever tasted.

name: Dark 85% Peru
tastingNotes: Espresso, blackberry, dark chocolate, earthy finish
description: For the purist. Deep and intense with a long, satisfying finish. Sourced from the Piura region.
process: Slow-fermented for 7 days, stone-ground for 96 hours, aged for 3 months.
pairing: Perfect with a smoky Islay Scotch or a dark roast Ethiopian coffee.
testimonial: A bold, uncompromising bar that demands to be savoured. Pure cacao artistry.

name: Milk & Sea Salt 40%
tastingNotes: Salted caramel, butter, toasted bread, creamy milk
description: A modern classic with hand‑harvested fleur de sel from the Guérande marshes.
process: Slowly conched for 48 hours, infused with Guérande sea salt flakes.
pairing: Pairs perfectly with a robust Cabernet Franc or a salted caramel latte.
testimonial: The salt crystals pop against the creamy chocolate – an explosion of flavour.

name: Dark 68% Guatemala
tastingNotes: Cinnamon, dried fruit, rich cocoa, subtle nutmeg
description: Warm and spicy. A tribute to the Maya tradition, sourced from the Lake Atitlán region.
process: Fermented for 6 days, dried in the sun, stone-ground for 72 hours.
pairing: Pairs wonderfully with a spiced rum or a chai latte.
testimonial: The warm spices transport you to the highlands of Guatemala. Truly authentic.

name: Dark 78% Venezuela
tastingNotes: Nutty, rich, dark chocolate, subtle hazelnut
description: A classic from the Orinoco Delta. Full-bodied with a creamy texture.
process: Slow-fermented for 7 days, hand-roasted, conched for 72 hours.
pairing: Pairs beautifully with a dark rum or a hazelnut latte.
testimonial: The hazelnut notes shine through – a luxurious, velvety experience.

name: Ruby 47% Brazil
tastingNotes: Berry, fruity, bright acidity, floral finish
description: A ruby chocolate with natural berry notes. Sourced from the Bahia region.
process: Unique fermentation process to preserve the ruby hue, conched for 48 hours.
pairing: Pairs wonderfully with a fruity Rosé or fresh strawberries.
testimonial: The berry notes are naturally occurring – it's a marvel of cacao engineering.

name: Dark 88% Dominican Republic
tastingNotes: Powerful, earthy, dark cocoa, woody undertones
description: For the true connoisseur. Uncompromising intensity from the Sánchez region.
process: Fermented for 8 days, sun-dried, stone-ground for 96 hours.
pairing: Perfect with a vintage Port or a dark espresso.
testimonial: Bold, intense, and unapologetic. A testament to the Dominican terroir.

name: Milk 50% Tanzania
tastingNotes: Honey, malt, creamy milk, subtle caramel
description: Smooth and mellow with honeyed notes. Sourced from the Kilimanjaro region.
process: Gently conched for 36 hours, hand-tempered.
pairing: Pairs beautifully with a honeyed white wine or a vanilla latte.
testimonial: The honey notes are sublime – a beautifully balanced milk chocolate.

name: Dark 70% Colombia
tastingNotes: Citrus, floral, bright acidity, lingering cocoa
description: Vibrant and fruity with a citrus twist. Sourced from the Sierra Nevada.
process: Fermented for 5 days, dried on bamboo mats, conched for 60 hours.
pairing: Pairs wonderfully with a citrusy Sauvignon Blanc or a light roast coffee.
testimonial: The bright citrus notes make this a refreshing and sophisticated choice.

name: Vegan Dark 65% Mexico
tastingNotes: Spicy, earthy, dark chocolate, subtle chili
description: A tribute to the Aztec tradition with a hint of chili. Sourced from the Oaxaca region.
process: Slow-fermented, stone-ground with organic cane sugar.
pairing: Pairs perfectly with a smoky mezcal or a Mexican hot chocolate.
testimonial: A delightful kick of chili that warms the soul. Vegan and incredible.

name: Single-Estate 75% Hawaii
tastingNotes: Tropical fruit, passionfruit, dark chocolate, floral finish
description: A rare single‑estate bar from the volcanic slopes of Hawaii. Truly unique.
process: Fermented for 7 days, hand-roasted, conched for 80 hours.
pairing: Pairs beautifully with a tropical cocktail or a macadamia nut tart.
testimonial: The tropical fruit notes are unlike anything I've ever tasted. A true treasure.

name: Dark 80% Trinidad & Tobago
tastingNotes: Complex, bold, dark fruit, tobacco finish
description: A complex bar with bold dark fruit notes. Sourced from the Maraval Valley.
process: Fermented for 8 days, sun-dried, conched for 84 hours.
pairing: Perfect with a full-bodied Shiraz or a dark rum.
testimonial: A connoisseur's dream – complex, bold, and unforgettable.

name: Milk 43% Belgium
tastingNotes: Classic velvety, creamy, milk chocolate, smooth finish
description: A classic Belgian milk chocolate with a velvety texture. The ultimate comfort bar.
process: Traditional Belgian conching for 48 hours.
pairing: Pairs beautifully with a cappuccino or a Belgian waffle.
testimonial: The creamy smoothness is simply divine. A timeless classic.

name: White & Raspberry 35%
tastingNotes: Summer blend, raspberry, creamy white chocolate, tangy finish
description: A refreshing summer blend with real raspberry pieces. Sourced from French cocoa butter.
process: Gently conched with freeze-dried raspberry powder.
pairing: Pairs perfectly with a Rosé Champagne or fresh strawberries.
testimonial: The tangy raspberry perfectly balances the sweetness – a summer delight.

name: Dark 90% Ecuador
tastingNotes: Pure cacao intensity, bitter, earthy, dark cocoa
description: Pure cacao intensity for the true purist. Sourced from the Amazon basin.
process: Fermented for 8 days, stone-ground for 100 hours, aged for 6 months.
pairing: Perfect with a vintage Port or a dark espresso.
testimonial: A rare and powerful experience. This is chocolate in its purest form.

name: Caramelized Milk 47%
tastingNotes: Salted butter caramel, creamy milk, toasted sugar
description: A decadent caramelized milk chocolate with hand-made salted caramel pieces.
process: Gently caramelized with French butter, conched for 60 hours.
pairing: Pairs wonderfully with a salted caramel latte or a robust stout.
testimonial: The salted caramel pieces are divine – a perfect balance of sweet and salty.

name: Dark 72% Jamaica
tastingNotes: Spiced rum notes, dark fruit, chocolate, warm finish
description: A deeply complex bar with warm spiced rum notes. Sourced from the Blue Mountains.
process: Fermented in Jamaica, conched with a hint of rum essence.
pairing: Pairs beautifully with aged rum or a spiced hot chocolate.
testimonial: The rum notes add a unique depth – a truly sophisticated bar.

name: Limited Edition 82% Peru
tastingNotes: Spring 2025 harvest, floral, blackberry, dark chocolate
description: A limited edition release from the 2025 Piura harvest. Rare and exquisite.
process: Limited production, stone-ground for 96 hours, aged for 6 months.
pairing: Perfect with a vintage Cabernet Sauvignon.
testimonial: The 2025 harvest is the finest I've ever tasted. An absolute must-buy.

name: Dark 66% Ghana
tastingNotes: Classic African, earthy, dark cocoa, mild fruit
description: A robust African chocolate with classic cocoa notes. Sourced from the Ashanti region.
process: Fermented for 5 days, sun-dried, conched for 60 hours.
pairing: Pairs beautifully with a black coffee or a dark stout.
testimonial: A beautifully balanced bar that showcases the best of African cacao.

name: Milk 41% India
tastingNotes: Kerala spice, cardamom, creamy milk, floral
description: A unique milk chocolate with a hint of cardamom. Sourced from Kerala.
process: Infused with organic cardamom, conched for 48 hours.
pairing: Pairs wonderfully with chai tea or a vanilla latte.
testimonial: The cardamom adds an exotic touch – a delightful sensory journey.

name: Dark 77% Papua New Guinea
tastingNotes: Tropical, smoky, dark chocolate, long finish
description: An adventurous bar with tropical and smoky notes. Sourced from the highlands.
process: Fermented for 7 days, smoked over local wood, conched for 72 hours.
pairing: Perfect with a peaty Islay Scotch or a dark espresso.
testimonial: A surprising and delightful complexity – perfect for the adventurous palate.

name: Valrhona-Style 58% Dark
tastingNotes: Rich, balanced, dark chocolate, classic French, smooth
description: A classic French dark chocolate with a rich and balanced flavor. Inspired by the great French chocolatiers.
process: French-style conching for 72 hours, hand-tempered.
pairing: Pairs beautifully with a Bordeaux or a French coffee.
testimonial: A masterpiece of balance – the perfect everyday luxury.
